title: Insula neuroanatomical networks predict interoceptive awareness.

assets.abstract: Interoceptive awareness (IA), the subjective and conscious perception of visceral and physiological signals from the body, has been associated with functions of cortical and subcortical neural systems involved in emotion control, mood and anxiety disorders. We recently hypothesized that IA and its contributions to mental health are realized by a brain interoception network (BIN) linking brain regions that receive ascending interoceptive information from the brainstem, such as the amygdala, insul

phase_mapping.bridge_rationale: Interoception — visceral perception, cardiac interoception, insula, vagal signaling, felt aliveness.
